source_jobs.title: Security Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We are looking for a passionate and experienced Senior Information Security Engineer to join our top-tier tech team. You will be a key member of our security function, responsible for designing, implementing, and continuously improving security across our global multi-cloud environment. You will not only respond to security incidents but also drive forward-looking security strategies and technical innovation.



Key Responsibilities

 - Threat Modeling & Incident Response: Build and maintain global cloud security incident response mechanisms, develop clear playbooks, and lead regular drills. Quickly handle complex security incidents, perform root cause analysis (RCA), and implement preventive measures.

 - Security Architecture & Operations: Design, deploy, and maintain security solutions across multi-cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure), ensuring architecture is scalable, resilient, and future-proof.

 - Enterprise Security Leadership: Drive security strategy alignment with business processes, continuously raising organizational security maturity.

 - Compliance & Automation: Ensure security practices meet international standards such as ISO 27001, ISO 27701, SOC 2. Promote security automation and “Security as Code” initiatives to improve operational efficiency.

 - Vulnerability Management & Penetration Testing: Conduct regular vulnerability scans, risk assessments, and penetration tests. Track emerging threats and coordinate remediation efforts.



Qualifications

Required:

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or related field, with 3+ years of experience in information security.

 - Strong expertise in cloud security, with hands-on experience in at least two major cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) and native security tools (e.g., AWS Security Hub, GCP Security Command Center).

 - Solid Linux system security skills, including hardening, log analysis, intrusion detection, and incident response.

 - Proven ability to handle high-pressure security incidents and perform clear post-incident reviews.

 - Strong knowledge of web and application security, with experience in penetration testing and code audits.

Preferred:

 - Recognized contributions to bug bounty programs or high-quality vulnerability reports.

 - Strong performance in CTF competitions or deep interest in offensive/defensive security research.

 - Professional certifications such as CISSP, CISA, OSCP, AWS/GCP/Azure security certifications.

 - Experience developing security tools or automation scripts using Python, Go, or similar languages.

source_jobs.title: Data Analyst
source_jobs.description:
About the Role
At Manus, we are leveraging data to build the future of AI-powered productivity. We are seeking a commercially-minded Data Analyst to join our team and partner with business leaders, product managers, and engineers. In this role, you will be at the heart of our decision-making process, translating complex data into actionable insights that drive product strategy, accelerate growth, and enhance user experience. If you are passionate about understanding users and using data to influence business outcomes in a fast-paced AI environment, this is the perfect opportunity for you.

Core Responsibilities
Business & Growth Analysis

- Analyze key business metrics, including revenue, cost, and profitability, to provide a clear view of business performance and inform strategic planning.

- Conduct in-depth analysis of user acquisition, retention, and expansion patterns to identify growth levers and optimize marketing strategies.

- Develop and maintain dashboards and reports that provide actionable insights to stakeholders across the company.

Product & User Insights

- Design and analyze A/B tests to evaluate the performance of new features and product changes, providing data-driven recommendations for product development.

- Conduct deep-dive analyses into user behavior to understand how users interact with our product, identifying pain points and opportunities for improvement.

- Perform cohort analysis and user segmentation to uncover trends in user engagement and value, informing targeted product and marketing initiatives.



We're Looking For Someone Who Has

Business Acumen

- With understanding of business dynamics and the ability to connect data with action.

- Strong commercial awareness, with experience in SaaS business models, user operations, or AI products being a significant advantage.

Analytical & Technical Skills

- Proficiency in SQL for complex data querying and experience with Python for data analysis and automation.

- Experience in using data visualization tools to create insightful and easy-to-understand dashboards.

Collaboration & Communication

- Excellent communication and presentation skills, with the ability to articulate complex analytical findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.

- A collaborative mindset and the ability to work effectively in a cross-functional team environment, partnering with product, engineering, and business teams.



Bonus Points

- With experience in a business analysis, product analysis, or data analysis role, preferably in a SaaS, AI, or technology company.

- Experience with AI-powered products.

source_jobs.title: Business Development Representative (BDR) Manager
source_jobs.description:
BDR MANAGER

We are seeking a driven and highly structured BDR Manager to join our team in Singapore and build our Business Development function across APAC. This is a foundational Go-To-Market (GTM) role, focused on pipeline creation, lead qualification, and building a world-class BDR engine from the ground up.

You will be hands-on in the early days—running inbound and outbound qualification calls yourself—while also owning the processes, SOPs, and team management required to scale a high-performing BDR organization. This role sits at the front line of our growth motion and is critical to how Manus converts demand into qualified revenue opportunities.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

As the BDR Manager at Manus, you will own the entire top-of-funnel motion and be accountable for consistent, high-quality pipeline generation for the sales team.


PIPELINE & QUALIFICATION OWNERSHIP

 - Run inbound and outbound qualification calls: Personally handle discovery and qualification calls across inbound leads (PLGs, demos, partnerships) and outbound prospects to set the quality bar.

 - Own lead qualification standards: Define and enforce qualification frameworks (e.g. ICP, MEDDICC-lite, PQL criteria) to ensure only sales-ready opportunities move forward.

 - Convert demand into pipeline: Turn marketing, product-led, partner, and outbound signals into well-qualified SQLs for Account Executives.


OUTBOUND & INBOUND MOTIONS

 - Build outbound prospecting playbooks: Define ICPs, personas, messaging, sequences, and call scripts across SMB, mid-market, and enterprise segments.

 - Scale inbound qualification workflows: Design fast, consistent processes for routing, prioritizing, and qualifying inbound leads to maximize speed-to-contact and conversion.

 - Collaborate with Marketing & Growth: Align closely on campaigns, experiments, and feedback loops to continuously improve lead quality and conversion rates.


BDR TEAM LEADERSHIP & MANAGEMENT

 - Build and manage the BDR team: Hire, onboard, coach, and manage a growing team of BDRs across inbound and outbound motions.

 - Set targets and performance metrics: Own activity, conversion, and pipeline KPIs; run weekly reviews and performance coaching.

 - Develop BDR career paths: Create clear progression frameworks from BDR → Senior BDR → AE or Sales roles.


PROCESS, SOPS & SALES INFRASTRUCTURE

 - Build SOPs from 0–1: Document and operationalize end-to-end BDR workflows—lead intake, qualification calls, handoffs, follow-ups, and CRM hygiene.

 - Own CRM and tooling setup: Define fields, stages, dashboards, and reporting to ensure data quality and visibility.

 - Drive continuous improvement: Use data and call insights to refine scripts, objection handling, and qualification criteria over time.


CUSTOMER & PRODUCT FEEDBACK LOOP

 - Be the first voice of the customer: Surface recurring objections, use cases, and buying signals from qualification calls.

 - Partner with Product & GTM: Translate frontline insights into actionable feedback for product roadmap, pricing, and GTM strategy.


WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR


EXPERIENCE & SKILLS

 - 3+ years of experience in B2B SaaS business development, sales development, or inside sales.

 - Proven track record running high-volume inbound and outbound qualification calls.

 - Experience building or leading a BDR team, including coaching, performance management, and hiring.

 - Strong understanding of sales qualification frameworks, pipeline metrics, and funnel optimization.

 - Comfortable selling and qualifying technical or AI-driven products; able to quickly grasp product value and customer workflows.

 - Experience designing SOPs, playbooks, and CRM processes in fast-growing environments.

 - Familiarity with APAC markets is a strong plus.


MINDSET & APPROACH

 - A builder’s mindset—you enjoy creating structure where none exists.

 - Highly organized, process-driven, and detail-oriented, without losing commercial intuition.

 - Hands-on leader who leads by ex

source_jobs.title: GTM Lead
source_jobs.description:
Why this role exists — the Big Challenges

Manus is a Benchmark backed startup, launched the first general AI agent in March and our product is now being used by millions of people around the world, generating $90mm in revenue.

Your mission is to lead our growth by strengthening our product led growth and building better capability to serve companies and teams. You will be building out a sales process from 0-1, working with different buyer personas, and building towards enterprise readiness.



What you'll do


NURTURE AND EXPAND EXISTING ACCOUNTS

 - Build systematic expansion playbooks that turn early adopters into strategic partners with growing ARR

 - Analyze user cohorts to identify expansion patterns and friction points


BUILD SCALABLE PLG MOTION AND OPTIMIZE CUSTOMER JOURNEY

 - Launch self-serve purchase and expansion paths

 - Design onboarding optimization experiments with rapid iteration cycles

 - Create systems, processes, and content that enable product-qualified leads to convert with minimal friction


CREATE SALES PROCESS AND INFRASTRUCTURE

 - Build out the GTM function from 0-1, from sales materials, processes, to personas and motions

 - Create experiments that you can measure and drive early wins and outcomes


SCALE TOWARDS ENTERPRISE READINESS

 - Work with product to develop infrastructure for enterprise deployment and support

 - Build towards enterprise readiness and start outbound and coverage of larger accounts

 - Start outbound motion and coverage of larger accounts

We want people that…

 - Entrepreneurial sellers. You've built 1→10 growth engines, and can build processes, discover customers from scratch

 - Think product-first. You are deeply curious, and have good product understanding, and can be an advocate of our product for customers

 - Customer first. You can connect with customers, understand their needs and pain points deeply

 - Build for leverage. You create self-serve content, automated workflows, and repeatable plays that scale beyond your personal capacity

 - Balance autonomy with collaboration. You can own an entire growth motion solo, but actively build cross-functional alignment

Nice-to-haves (but not deal-breakers)

 - 5–8 yrs in growth roles at PLG SaaS companies

 - Experience scaling from $1M→$10M ARR with strong net revenue retention

 - Track record building customer education programs and in-product growth loops

What we offer

 - Competitive startup equity + salary pegged to top-quartile SaaS benchmarks

 - Greenfield ownership: design expansion playbooks, choose tooling, build v1 team

 - Direct line to founders & product; your insights ship within days, not quarters

 - Budget for customer programs, tools, and anything that drives expansion faster

Compensation

$130,000 - $250,000 + Proposed Equity

source_jobs.title: SEO Content Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Job Overview:
We are seeking an SEO Content Specialist who is passionate about content and curious about new technologies. As a core member of our SEO team, you will focus on creating high-quality content that is directly tied to our product. You will need a deep understanding of SEO strategy, exceptional content taste, and strong editorial judgment. Whether creating content yourself or reviewing external submissions, you will be the guardian of our standards, ensuring everything we publish is optimized for search engines while reflecting our brand's unique value.
If you are familiar with SaaS products and skilled in using AI tools to enhance content creation, you are exactly who we're looking for. If you're not familiar with these yet, that's okay too! As long as you have a strong curiosity and a rapid learning ability, we are excited to provide you with the opportunity to grow and master them with us.

Key Responsibilities:

 1. Write compelling, conversion-focused copy for landing pages and in-depth blog posts that support our latest product features and industry trends.

 2. Develop content strategies based on keyword research and user intent, applying SEO best practices to all content to improve search rankings.

 3. Act as the team's "gatekeeper of content taste," reviewing all internal and external content to ensure it meets our high standards for quality, tone, and accuracy.

 4. Proactively explore and apply AI and efficiency SaaS tools to streamline the content workflow and enhance team agility and output.

 5. Collaborate closely with marketing, design, and product teams to ensure content strategy aligns with overall company goals and contributes to a seamless user experience.
    

Qualifications:

 - 2+ years of experience in SEO content creation, content marketing, or copywriting, with a strong portfolio of work (please include links to landing pages, blog articles, etc.).

 - Exceptional writing, editing, and proofreading skills with a high standard for content quality and attention to detail.

 - A strong understanding of SEO, including keyword research, on-page SEO, and content marketing strategies.

 - A strong interest in and an excellent ability to quickly learn about SaaS products, AI tools, and related technologies.

 - Excellent project management skills and the ability to independently drive content projects from ideation to publication.

 - (Bonus Points) Experience with AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT, Manus, Jasper, Midjourney) or efficiency SaaS tools.

source_jobs.title: Social Media Operation Intern
source_jobs.description:
About the role
We’re looking for someone who lives and breathes X (Twitter) and has the curiosity to know what’s trending before it hits the timeline. As our Professional Doomscroller, you’ll be the eyes and ears of Manus—spotting conversations we should join, surfacing insights from the AI/startup/tech community, and engaging with people in a way that’s sharp, human, and fun. This role isn’t about posting generic replies. It’s about crafting witty, thoughtful, and context-aware comments that make people stop scrolling and pay attention. You’ll help us build Manus’ voice on X as the “action engine” in AI, while learning how brands grow influence through social listening and community-driven engagement.

What you’ll do
- Monitor X for conversations around AI, startups, productivity, and tech—surfacing the right ones for us to engage in.
- Write and post witty, insightful replies under Manus’ account to amplify our presence and grow our network.
- Track what people are saying about Manus and related products—turning chatter into actionable insights.
- Spot cultural moments, memes, or debates we can join in real-time.
- Collaborate with our marketing team to shape Manus’ tone of voice and engagement strategy.

What you’ll learn
- How to grow a brand on X through community-led engagement.
- The ins and outs of social listening tools and workflows.
- How to translate trends into content and campaigns. - What it takes to build and protect a startup’s reputation online.

What we’re looking for
- You’re online a lot (really, a lot) and you actually enjoy it.
- You understand the cultural nuances of X—tech bros, AI skeptics, VCs, founders, builders, and meme accounts.
- You have a sharp, witty writing style that fits in with internet culture.
- You’re curious, fast, and unafraid to experiment.
- Bonus if you’ve already built an engaged following yourself.

- Collaborate with the creative team to develop and test ad copy, visuals, and landing pages.

Requirements:

- Strong understanding of the ad trafficking process, from creative submission to campaign launch.

- A good sense of what makes ad creatives effective, with experience in A/B testing and creative optimization.

- Excellent data analysis skills with the ability to interpret metrics, identify trends, and make data-driven decisions.

- A proactive and detail-oriented approach to problem-solving.

source_jobs.title: Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
JOB SUMMARY

We are looking for an experienced Data Engineer to take ownership of our data infrastructure's efficiency, performance, and reliability. The ideal candidate will be responsible for auditing our current systems, identifying areas for improvement, and implementing robust solutions to ensure our data is both performant and trustworthy. This role is critical in establishing a "ground truth" data source that will empower our data analytics and data science teams to derive actionable insights with confidence. You will be instrumental in building and maintaining a scalable and resilient data ecosystem that supports the company's strategic objectives.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Infrastructure Auditing & Optimization

- Conduct comprehensive audits of the existing data infrastructure to assess its efficiency, scalability, and performance.

- Identify and analyze performance bottlenecks, and propose and implement optimization strategies.

- Re-design and modernize data pipelines for greater scalability and reduced latency.

- Implement monitoring and alerting systems to proactively identify and address infrastructure issues.

Data Pipeline & ETL Development

- Design, build, and maintain robust and scalable ETL/ELT processes to ingest data from a wide variety of sources.

- Assemble large, complex datasets that meet both functional and non-functional business requirements.

- Automate manual data processes to improve efficiency and reduce the potential for human error.

Ground Truth Data Source Management

- Establish and maintain a centralized, reliable source of truth for all key business data.

- Implement rigorous data quality checks and validation processes to ensure data accuracy and consistency.

- Develop and enforce data governance best practices, including data lineage and metadata management.

Collaboration & Support

- Work closely with data scientists, data analysts, and other stakeholders to understand their data requirements and provide them with the data they need.

- Build analytical tools and provide technical support to assist teams in leveraging the data infrastructure effectively.

- Act as a subject matter expert on data engineering best practices and advocate for their adoption across the organization.




SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS

The following skills and qualifications are required for this role:

Educational Background: A Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field.

Professional Experience: A minimum of 3-5 years of hands-on experience in a data engineering role.

Technical Proficiencies:

•Programming: Advanced proficiency in Python, with experience in either Java or Scala being a plus.

•SQL: Expertise in writing complex, highly-optimized SQL queries across large datasets.

•Cloud Platforms: Demonstrable experience with at least one major cloud platform, such as AWS (Redshift, S3, EC2), Google Cloud Platform (BigQuery, Dataproc), or Azure.

•Big Data Technologies: Hands-on experience with big data tools like Apache Spark, Hadoop, and Kafka.

•Data Warehousing: Experience with modern data warehousing solutions such as Snowflake, Redshift, or BigQuery.

Soft Skills:

•Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills.

•Strong communication and collaboration abilities, with a knack for explaining complex technical concepts to non-technical audiences.

•A proactive and self-motivated work ethic, with a strong sense of ownership and a commitment to delivering high-quality results.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

While not mandatory, the following qualifications will be highly regarded:

•A Master's degree in a relevant technical field.

•Professional certifications in cloud technologies (e.g., AWS Certified Data Analytics, Google Professional Data Engineer).

•Experience with data orchestration tools such as Airflow or Prefect.

•Familiarity with containerization technologies like Docker and Kubernetes.

•A solid understanding of data modeling principles and best pra

source_jobs.title: Marketing Intern

We are looking for a highly motivated and enthusiastic Influencer Engagement Intern to join our remote team. source_jobs.title: Visual Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

At Manus AI, we are shaping the future of human-computer interaction, and our brand's visual identity is central to how we communicate our vision to the world. We are looking for a creative and passionate Visual Designer to join our team. This role is dedicated to crafting compelling visual experiences that elevate our brand and effectively communicate our product's value across all marketing channels.

You will be a key player in our creative process, responsible for designing stunning visuals for marketing campaigns, product promotions, and brand initiatives. The ideal candidate has a strong international design perspective, a keen eye for detail, and a passion for exploring new creative frontiers while maintaining brand consistency.




RESPONSIBILITIES

•Marketing & Product Visuals: Take charge of visual design for online marketing campaigns and product feature promotions, creating engaging and effective creative assets.

•Brand Consistency & Evolution: Maintain and refine our brand guidelines, ensuring a consistent and high-quality visual identity across all channels and touchpoints.

•Trend Analysis & Visual Standards: Stay ahead of industry design trends, participate in defining the standards for our overall visual language, and continuously elevate the quality and impact of our external communications.

•Global Design Perspective: Possess a strong international design vision and aesthetic sense. Insightfully analyze design trends and understand the aesthetic preferences of our global target audience.

•Creative Exploration: Maintain brand consistency across all design outputs while constantly exploring new creative directions and innovative visual solutions.


REQUIREMENTS

•Educational Background: Bachelor's degree or higher in Design, Visual Communication, or a related field.

•Software Proficiency: Expert-level proficiency in design software such as Photoshop, Illustrator, and Figma.

•AI Tool Mastery: Skilled in using AI-powered tools to enhance the creative process and generate innovative design assets.


PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS (BONUS POINTS)

•Illustration & Drawing Skills: Strong capabilities in illustration and hand-drawing.

•Motion Design Experience: Experience with video production or motion graphics design.

•Event & Merchandise Design: Previous work experience in event visual design, merchandise, or other brand collateral design.

source_jobs.title: DevOps & SRE Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Key Responsibilities

Cluster Operations & Management

 - Manage and maintain container clusters (Kubernetes, Docker) and open-source component clusters (Kafka, Redis, Elasticsearch) across multiple business units

 - Ensure optimal performance, scalability, and reliability of distributed systems

Infrastructure Platform Development

 - Design, build, and enhance infrastructure operation platforms

 - Develop and maintain systems for infrastructure management, CI/CD pipelines, monitoring/alerting, and centralized logging

 - Drive platform standardization and automation initiatives

High Availability & Reliability

 - Ensure maximum uptime for production services through proactive monitoring and incident response

 - Continuously optimize service architecture, deployment strategies, and operational processes

 - Implement and maintain SLA/SLO frameworks and reliability engineering practices

Automation & Process Improvement

 - Lead the development of automated operations and maintenance systems

 - Create self-service tools and workflows to improve team productivity

 - Establish best practices for infrastructure such as code and configuration management



Required Qualifications

Experience & Education

 - 2+ years of hands-on experience in Systems Operations, DevOps, or Site Reliability Engineering (SRE)

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related technical field preferred

Cloud & Infrastructure

 - Experience with public cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) is highly valued

 - Strong understanding of large-scale internet architecture and distributed systems

 - Proven experience with infrastructure monitoring, logging, and observability tools

Technical Skills

 - Proficiency in scripting and automation using Shell, Python, or similar languages

 - Strong knowledge of containerization technologies (Kubernetes, Docker)

 - Hands-on experience operating production-grade container clusters and managing CI/CD pipelines

 - Strong familiarity with common infrastructure components: Nginx, MySQL, Redis, Kafka, Elasticsearch



Advanced Networking (Preferred)

source_jobs.title: Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
We're seeking a skilled Backend Engineer to join our growing team and drive the development of Manus's core infrastructure and business systems. This role offers the opportunity to work across two specialized tracks: infrastructure engineering and business application development, allowing you to shape both the technical foundation and user-facing features of our AI-powered platform.



Key Responsibilities

Core Backend Development

 - Build high-performance, scalable backend services using Golang to support core AI product features

 - Design, develop, and optimize APIs (RESTful and GraphQL) ensuring efficient frontend-backend interactions

 - Participate in system architecture design to ensure stability, reliability, and scalability at enterprise scale

 - Handle large-scale data flows and optimize distributed systems for peak performance

 - Collaborate closely with frontend and AI agent teams to support rapid product iteration cycles

 - Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code following industry best practices



Infrastructure Track (Option A)

Middleware & Core Systems Management

 - Maintain and optimize Manus's foundational middleware stack including databases, caching layers, logging systems, and message queues

 - Design and manage Kubernetes cluster architecture for optimal resource utilization and fault tolerance

 - Implement monitoring, alerting, and observability solutions across all infrastructure components

 - Ensure high availability and disaster recovery capabilities for mission-critical systems

E2B Cluster Operations

 - Oversee maintenance, configuration, and enhancement of Manus's E2B cluster infrastructure

 - Drive capacity planning and scaling initiatives to meet growing demand

 - Implement cost optimization strategies while maintaining performance standards

 - Collaborate with DevOps teams to streamline deployment and operational workflows



Business Systems Track (Option B)

Core Business Development

 - Develop and maintain user management systems, authentication, and authorization frameworks

 - Build integrations with marketing automation platforms and operational tools

 - Create robust APIs for customer onboarding, engagement tracking, and lifecycle management

 - Implement analytics and reporting systems for business intelligence

Payment & Monetization Platform

 - Design and develop comprehensive payment processing systems with multiple gateway integrations

 - Build credit and points management systems with real-time balance tracking

 - Implement coupon, discount, and promotional campaign management features

 - Develop membership and subscription management capabilities with automated billing

 - Ensure PCI compliance and implement fraud detection mechanisms



Required Qualifications

 - Bachelor's degree or higher in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or related technical field

 - Minimum 3 years of backend development experience with demonstrated expertise in Golang

 - Strong understanding of microservices architecture and service orchestration tools (gRPC, Kubernetes)

 - Proficiency in database design, optimization, and management (MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB)

 - Hands-on experience with distributed systems, message queues (Kafka, RabbitMQ), and caching technologies (Redis)

 - Solid understanding of cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure) and containerization technologies

 - Experience with CI/CD pipelines and infrastructure-as-code practices

 - Strong problem-solving abilities and passion for learning new technologies



Preferred Qualifications

 - Previous experience in backend development for AI, ML, or SaaS products

 - Experience with payment processing systems, financial transactions, or fintech applications

 - Knowledge of infrastructure monitoring tools (Prometheus, Grafana, ELK stack)

 - Familiarity with security best practices and compliance frameworks

 - Experience working in international, distributed teams

 - Contributions to open-source proj

source_jobs.title: GTM Intern
source_jobs.description:
About the Role
We're looking for a driven and proactive GTM Intern to join our team and help accelerate Manus's market expansion. This role is ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of business strategy, customer engagement, product marketing, and community building. You'll work directly on initiatives that drive customer acquisition, partnership development and market positioning—gaining hands-on experience in building go-to-market motions from the ground up at a cutting-edge AI startup.

Responsibilities
Customer & Partner Engagement:
•Drive customer and partner outreach: Identify, research, and engage with potential customers, partners, and strategic accounts via email, LinkedIn, and calls.
•Own the follow-up and relationship-building process: Maintain engagement momentum through timely follow-ups, video calls, and meetings.
•Conduct business solutioning and product positioning: Work closely with prospects and partners to understand their pain points, explore how Manus can address their needs, and develop compelling use cases that demonstrate clear value and ROI.
•Support sales and partnership discussions: Engage with business executives, decision-makers, and technical stakeholders to align on objectives, scope opportunities, and support deal progression.
Community & Events:
•Plan and execute community events: Take ownership of the full event lifecycle—from concept to execution.
•Manage event operations: Handle the tactical details that make events successful.
•Build and nurture community: Develop and implement strategies to grow and engage the Manus user community.
•Foster community connections: Create opportunities for community members to connect with each other and with the Manus team, including organizing community challenges, facilitating peer-to-peer learning sessions, highlighting community success stories, and building programs that recognize and reward active contributors.
Operations & Enablement:
•Manage CRM and pipelines: Track and manage leads, opportunities, and partnership pipelines using CRM tools, ensuring data accuracy and follow-through.
•Design customer success playbooks: Help develop scalable onboarding playbooks, tutorials, guides, and customer education materials that drive product adoption and satisfaction.
•Collaborate cross-functionally: Partner with Product, Marketing, Sales, and Growth teams to ensure alignment on positioning, messaging, and strategic priorities.
•Bridge product capabilities with market needs: Develop a strong understanding of Manus's product features, technical architecture, and positioning to confidently map solutions to customer and partner challenges.


Requirements
Education & Background:
•Currently pursuing or recently completed a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business, Marketing, Economics, Computer Science, or a related field.
Core Skills:
•Excellent communication skills: Comfortable engaging with business executives and technical stakeholders across email, video calls, and in-person meetings.
•Technical curiosity: Able to discuss both business value and technical considerations in customer and partnership contexts.
•Highly organized and proactive: Capable of managing multiple pipelines, projects, and stakeholders simultaneously with attention to detail
•Self-starter mentality with eagerness to learn in a fast-moving startup environment.
•Passion for AI, technology, and startups.
Bonus
•Prior internship or project experience in B2B sales, partnerships, go-to-market strategy, growth marketing, consulting, or VC.
•Experience using Manus or familiarity with AI agent platforms.

•Hands-on GTM experience: Build go-to-market strategies from 0 to 1 in a fast-paced, high-growth AI startup environment.
•Direct mentorship: Work closely with experienced GTM, product, and leadership team members who will guide your professional development.
•Portfolio building: Create a portfolio of content, case studies, partnerships, and community initiatives you've
